key_highlights: Global tech sell-off intensifies, led by AI and chip stocks, with Alphabet tumbling after DeepMind loses Nobel laureate to Anthropic.
Oil prices fall below $75 for first time since March as Hormuz traffic recovers after US-Iran peace deal, easing geopolitical risk.
US stocks drift near records but mixed as tech weakness offsets energy gains; dollar strengthens to highest since November on Fed rate-hike bets.
AI's next bottleneck is power, with energy demand surging; Google and Energy Dome advance multi-continent energy storage buildout.
Alan Greenspan, former Fed chair, dies at 100, prompting reflection on monetary policy legacy.
SK Hynix tops Samsung to become South Korea's most valuable company amid AI memory demand.
Middle East conflict hit smaller construction companies hardest; maritime watchdog to evacuate stranded seafarers.
Trump proposes crop sales to Iran and citizenship fee hikes; Iran's unfrozen funds to remain under US control.
Eli Lilly CEO outlines plan to defeat pharma's boom-bust cycle; AbbVie wins FDA approval for Skinvive and EU approval for SKYRIZI.
SpaceX stock tumbles 23% from high; signs $6.3B computing deal with open-source AI startup Reflection.

sectors.trend: Sharp sell-off driven by AI valuation concerns, talent poaching (DeepMind loss), and profit-taking after recent highs. SK Hynix outperforms Samsung, but broader AI rout revives scrutiny of leveraged ETF boom in Korea.
sectors.implications: Near-term volatility likely; investors should differentiate between AI enablers (semiconductors, energy) and overhyped names. Long-term AI demand remains intact but power constraints become critical.

sectors.trend: Oil falls below $75 as Hormuz peace deal allows tanker traffic to resume, easing supply fears. Renewables and energy storage gain traction: Novva expands solar in Colombia, Google/Energy Dome build storage in Ireland, RWE acquires Amprion stake.
sectors.implications: Oil downside risk if peace holds; renewable energy and storage stocks benefit from structural demand and AI power needs. Energy security in Southeast Asia via electrification is a long-term theme.

sectors.trend: Dollar strengthens to highest since November on Fed rate-hike bets; euro weakest in a year as Lagarde diverges from Fed. Treasuries see good auction demand as rate-hike bets trimmed. Charles Schwab reports record May core net new assets.
sectors.implications: Strong dollar pressures EM currencies and stocks; US financials benefit from higher rates and asset inflows. Rate-hike expectations may cap equity upside.

sectors.trend: Biotech sees volatility: LSD-based depression drug drives stock surge; AbbVie wins FDA/EU approvals for Skinvive and SKYRIZI; Eli Lilly CEO outlines plan to manage boom-bust cycles. Incyte outperforms healthcare sector.
sectors.implications: Innovation in mental health and dermatology offers high-reward opportunities but regulatory risks remain. Large pharma with diversified pipelines (AbbVie, Lilly) are more resilient.

sectors.trend: Summer sales events kick off as inflation squeezes US consumers; Outback Steakhouse rival chain closes 24 locations; Heineken names coffee CEO to lead turnaround; Coke/Pepsi/Dr Pepper use QR codes for ingredients.
sectors.implications: Consumer discretionary faces margin pressure; discount retailers and value-oriented brands may benefit. Beverage companies adapt to transparency trends.

sectors.trend: Middle East conflict hit smaller construction companies hardest; Bristow acquires Berry Aviation in government push; Caterpillar remains a high-quality long-term buy.
sectors.implications: Defense and government-related industrials (Bristow) see demand; construction faces regional headwinds. Infrastructure spending and reshoring support Caterpillar.

key_themes: AI power bottleneck driving energy storage and renewable investments
Geopolitical détente (US-Iran) lowering oil risk but raising questions on Iran trade
Fed rate-hike expectations strengthening dollar and pressuring EM
Tech sell-off as AI hype meets reality (talent poaching, valuation concerns)
Consumer resilience tested by inflation, with summer sales as a barometer
Monetary policy legacy after Greenspan's death
Corporate tax policy uncertainty (CFO.com tax event)
Leveraged ETF risks in Korea and globally
Energy security via electrification in Southeast Asia
Biotech innovation in mental health and dermatology

risk_assessment.mitigation: Diversify into AI enablers (energy, semiconductors) and avoid overvalued AI names; consider hedging with put spreads on tech ETFs.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Oil price volatility from geopolitical shifts
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce exposure to oil producers; increase allocation to renewable energy and storage stocks. Monitor Hormuz traffic and Iran deal implementation.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Stronger dollar and Fed rate hikes
risk_assessment.mitigation: Underweight EM equities and currencies; favor US financials and dollar-denominated assets. Consider duration positioning in Treasuries if rate-hike bets are trimmed.
Focus on defensive consumer staples and discount retailers; avoid highly leveraged consumer discretionary names.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Middle East conflict escalation despite peace deal
risk_assessment.mitigation: Maintain some exposure to defense stocks (Bristow) and energy infrastructure; monitor maritime evacuations and refinery attacks.
Stay informed on corporate tax changes (CFO.com event) and student loan reforms; avoid sectors heavily exposed to policy shifts.

opportunities.recommendation: Increase allocation to renewable energy and energy storage stocks (e.g., First Solar, Energy Dome partners, RWE)
opportunities.rationale: AI power demand and geopolitical oil risks accelerate the transition; Novva, Google/Energy Dome, and RWE moves signal strong institutional commitment.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy SK Hynix on dips as AI memory demand leader
opportunities.rationale: SK Hynix tops Samsung in market cap, benefiting from HBM memory for AI; tech sell-off provides entry point.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in biotech with near-term catalysts (AbbVie, Incyte, psychedelic drug developers)
opportunities.rationale: FDA/EU approvals and positive trial results (LSD-based depression drug) drive stock gains; AbbVie's Skinvive and SKYRIZI approvals add revenue streams.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider long positions in US financials (Charles Schwab, JPMorgan) on rate-hike environment
opportunities.rationale: Record asset inflows and higher rates boost net interest margins; dollar strength supports US financials.

opportunities.recommendation: Explore opportunities in defense and government services (Bristow, Lockheed Martin)
opportunities.rationale: Middle East tensions and government push for aviation services support demand; Bristow's Berry Aviation acquisition aligns with this trend.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to overvalued tech/AI stocks and rotate into quality dividend growers (e.g., Caterpillar, Consolidated Edison)
defensive_moves.rationale: Tech sell-off may deepen; defensive industrials and utilities offer stable cash flows and dividends. Con Ed benefits from electrification trends.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge oil exposure with put options on crude or short oil ETFs
defensive_moves.rationale: Oil below $75 and Hormuz recovery suggest further downside; peace deal and Iran sanctions waiver reduce risk premium.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase cash or short-duration Treasuries to protect against rate-hike volatility
defensive_moves.rationale: Strong dollar and Fed rate-hike bets create bond market uncertainty; good auction demand suggests Treasuries are attractive at current yields.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid EM equities and currencies until dollar strength abates
defensive_moves.rationale: Strongest dollar since November pressures EM; India's Russian oil imports and Korea's leveraged ETF rout highlight vulnerability.

market_outlook.short_term: Markets are likely to remain volatile over the next 1-3 months as the tech sell-off continues to weigh on sentiment, while oil's decline provides a tailwind for consumer discretionary and transportation. The dollar's strength will pressure EM assets. Key focus will be on Fed rhetoric (rate-hike bets vs. data) and AI earnings (Micron, Nvidia). Geopolitical risks from Middle East and Russia-Ukraine (diesel export ban) remain but are partially priced in.
market_outlook.long_term: Over 6-12 months, the AI power bottleneck will drive significant investment in energy storage, renewables, and grid infrastructure, creating structural growth opportunities. The US-Iran peace deal could reshape oil markets and trade flows. Biotech innovation in mental health and dermatology offers high-reward potential. However, corporate tax changes, student loan reforms, and potential recession risks from Fed tightening warrant caution. The passing of Alan Greenspan may prompt policy reflection but unlikely to change near-term Fed stance.
key_catalysts: Micron earnings (this week) - bellwether for AI memory demand
PCE data (this week) - inflation gauge for Fed policy
US-Iran deal implementation and Hormuz traffic normalization
Fed speeches and rate decision in July
AI regulation developments (Trump proposal for US stake in AI firms)
Corporate tax reform outcomes from CFO.com event
Eli Lilly's strategy execution against pharma cycles
Korea AI rout and leveraged ETF market stability
watch_list: Oil price and Hormuz tanker traffic data
Dollar index and EM currency pairs (INR, KRW, BRL)
Tech ETF flows and volatility (QQQ, SMH)
Fed funds futures and rate-hike probability
AI power demand metrics (data center energy contracts)
Biotech trial results (psychedelic drugs, gene therapy)
Middle East construction and maritime evacuation progress
Consumer spending data (retail sales, summer sales performance)
Corporate bond issuance (Sony's US bond return)
Regulatory decisions on rent control, citizenship fees, and student loans
